heroHeadline: ORBÁN DEFEATED — Hungary's NATO Blocker Ousted After 16 Years; Magyar Pledges to Rebuild Alliance Ties Ahead of Helsingborg Summit
heroSubtitle: Viktor Orbán conceded Hungary's parliamentary election after 16 years in power, with Péter Magyar's Tisza party winning 138 of 199 seats. The result removes NATO's most obstructionist voice on Ukraine membership and burden-sharing, potentially unlocking the €90B EU loan to Ukraine and shifting alliance consensus ahead of the Helsingborg Foreign Ministers summit (May 21–22). Magyar has pledged to rebuild Hungary's frayed ties with NATO and the EU. Orbán's departure also severs Putin's main EU ally. The alliance crisis over US Article 5 reliability and Trump's withdrawal threats continues — but the Budapest veto bloc has been removed.
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, Wall Street Journal, The Economist, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, Axios, NPR, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, EU News, Swedish MFA, France 24, Euronews, CNN, PBS NewsHour
